stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The context is provided by Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/ML domain, specifically text generation using transformer architectures.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major players in the large language model space.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
This attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, particularly concerning safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: A 0-click exploit chain for the Pixel 10
stories.author: happyhardcore
bullet_points: The story reports the existence of a 0-click exploit chain.
The vulnerability specifically targets the Pixel 10 device.
This represents a significant security threat in mobile operating systems.

stories.title: Image-blaster: Creates 3D environments, SFX, and meshes from a single image
stories.author: MattRogish
bullet_points: Introduces 'Image-blaster,' a tool capable of generating complex 3D assets from a single input image.
The tool synthesizes not only 3D meshes but also associated sound effects (SFX) and environmental data.
Represents a significant advancement in generative AI for 3D content creation, potentially impacting gaming and VFX industries.

stories.title: linux 0-day, access root-owned files as an unprivileged user
stories.author: github.com via dzwdz
bullet_points: A Linux 0-day vulnerability has been discovered.
The exploit allows an unprivileged user to gain access to root-owned files.
This represents a critical security flaw impacting Linux systems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers and deepseek_v3 architectures.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline shown is for text-generation, indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance optimization.
The update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
This represents a significant step toward faster, optimized local AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is undergoing another executive reorganization, signaling strategic shifts within the company.
The company is prioritizing a 'go all-in' strategy focused specifically on developing AI agents.
Greg Brockman has been designated as the official lead for all product-related initiatives at OpenAI.

stories.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has updated its spam policy to specifically target attempts to 'manipulate' its AI models.
This policy update applies to content appearing in new features like AI Overview and AI Mode within Google Search.
The change reinforces Google's stance against deceptive techniques designed to artificially influence search rankings.

stories.title: OpenAI now wants ChatGPT to access your bank accounts
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: OpenAI is integrating direct bank account access into ChatGPT.
The feature utilizes Plaid, a platform connecting apps to over 12,000 financial institutions.
This development tests user trust in AI by allowing financial data access via the chatbot.

stories.title: OpenAI feels “burned” by Apple’s crappy ChatGPT integration, insiders say
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Legal action is forcing Apple to disclose internal communications regarding a ChatGPT integration deal.
The story suggests OpenAI perceives Apple's integration as substandard or detrimental to their interests.
This highlights ongoing competitive tensions and high-stakes negotiations between major tech players (OpenAI, Apple, Musk).

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static after pretraining, allowing for timely, domain-specific knowledge integration.
Encodes new knowledge into a dedicated memory model, offering an efficient alternative to full model fine-tuning.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new knowledge without retraining the core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely application.
The approach uses a dedicated memory model to efficiently encode domain-specific information into the existing LLM architecture.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard machine unlearning techniques when applied to quantized (compressed) language models.
It demonstrates a systematic dual failure where gradient-based forgetting methods are lost under 4-bit quantization, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
The research introduces 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' to address this practical deployment challenge, highlighting a significant hurdle in deploying truly forgettable AI.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology synthesizes findings from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to validate threat surface coverage.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available.
It is being deployed on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
The model is highlighted for being fast and cost-efficient.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models offline on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Andon Labs is conducting experiments where AI agents manage businesses autonomously.
The latest experiment involves four radio stations powered by major AI models (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok).
The premise suggests an examination of AI's limitations when operating without human oversight.

stories.title: Preprint server arXiv will ban submitters of AI-generated hallucinations
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: arXiv, a major preprint server, is implementing a new policy regarding AI-generated content.
The policy specifically targets and threatens to ban submitters who introduce 'hallucinations' (inaccurate AI outputs) into submissions.
This signals an increasing effort by academic platforms to combat misinformation and ensure scientific rigor in the age of generative AI.

stories.title: Making cement from a different type of rock could clean up emissions
stories.author: Scott K. Johnson
bullet_points: The article discusses alternative raw materials for producing Portland cement, moving beyond traditional limestone.
The core focus is on using different types of rock to potentially reduce the carbon emissions associated with cement production.
This represents a significant, albeit industrial, shift toward sustainable construction practices.

stories.title: Bill to block publishers from killing online games advances in California
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: California bill aims to prevent publishers from abruptly shutting down online games.
The legislation mandates that publishers must provide either an independent play patch or issue refunds following server shutdowns.
This represents a regulatory effort to protect consumers in the digital gaming industry.

stories.title: Honda shows off new hybrids for America as it absorbs $9 billion EV loss
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Honda is focusing on new hybrid models for the American market.
The automaker reported its first annual loss in over seven decades.
The financial struggles are linked to absorbing a significant $9 billion loss related to EVs.

stories.title: Rocket Report: Cowboy up for data centers in LEO; Russia's new ICBM actually works
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its first test flight on Tuesday, May 19.
The report covers the use of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for data center deployment.
The article also touches upon the successful testing of a new Russian Intercontinental Ballistic Missile (ICBM).

stories.title: Routine vaccines may cut dementia risk—experts have startling hypothesis on how
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Routine vaccinations may offer protection against dementia.
Experts propose a novel mechanism where vaccines train a previously thought 'untrainable' part of the immune system.
The research suggests a potential new avenue for preventative health interventions against neurodegenerative diseases.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more robust evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational challenges of visual reasoning in AI, contrasting direct image generation with alternative methods.
It compares two main approaches: agentic reasoning (using code/tools) and latent reasoning (using hidden embeddings).
A key trade-off identified is that agentic methods introduce latency due to external execution, while direct generation is costly.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying actual, chronological world events (like news articles) to provide realistic training data.
This allows agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic, interactive human instructions.
The approach moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies used in existing scene editing methods.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D geometric consistency of generative video models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders for video quality assessment.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and instability when co-training disparate Partial Differential Equation (PDE) regimes.
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands from different physical simulations (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media).

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: OpenDeepThink introduces a new framework to improve LLM reasoning by scaling computation breadth rather than just depth.
It addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to choose the best candidate.
The method aims to overcome the limitations of noisy and biased pointwise LLM judging during test-time inference.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways for early disease detection.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of temporal precision in clinical data by combining unstructured text and structured EHR data.
It proposes a Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment approach to reconstruct accurate clinical timelines for patient trajectory modeling.
The application is highly relevant to complex medical conditions like sepsis, aiming to improve risk forecasting.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are fundamentally limited in verifying complex AI safety claims.
Current AI governance frameworks demand evidence of properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded capability.
Existing assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are epistemically constrained by observable model outputs.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architectural enhancement to ensure the video decoder receives equal conditioning to the denoising network.
Aims to improve the structural integrity and detail consistency of generated video content.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, open-ended environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically to provide realistic training and evaluation scenarios.
Agents use FutureSim to forecast future world events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with a time-ordered stream of real data.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing neural network components.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Aims to advance mechanistic interpretability by providing a rigorous way to verify if two network parts compute the same function.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The core problem is gradient conflict when co-training models for disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes using a Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing mechanism to overcome the limitations of dense neural operators.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes using a Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment approach to combine unstructured text (narratives) and structured data (EHRs).
The goal is to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting in complex conditions like sepsis by resolving temporal ambiguity.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods cannot fully verify the safety claims mandated by modern AI governance frameworks.
AI governance demands verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited by what is observable in model outputs.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The paper addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models when performing dexterous manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch during human takeover in Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands.
The proposed 'Hand-in-the-Loop' method aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces 'FutureSim,' a new grounded simulation framework for testing AI agents.
The system replays real-world events chronologically to evaluate an agent's ability to adapt to new information.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with historical data.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper investigates how different retrieval strategies impact agentic search systems powered by LLMs.
It addresses a gap in literature regarding the interaction between retrieval choice, agent architecture, and tool-calling.
The research focuses on complex agentic workflows where LLMs autonomously retrieve, reason, and use tools.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal approach (Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment) to combine unstructured narrative data with structured EHR data.
The goal is to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting for complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current evaluations where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is crucial for complex tasks.

stories.title: U.S. DOJ demands Apple and Google unmask over 100k users of car-tinkering app
stories.author: tencentshill
bullet_points: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) is demanding that Apple and Google reveal the identities of over 100,000 users.
The users in question are associated with a car-tinkering application.
This action highlights ongoing legal tensions between government surveillance requests and tech company user privacy policies.

stories.title: Show HN: Watch a neural net learn to play Snake
stories.author: c1b
bullet_points: Demonstrates a neural network learning to play Snake directly in the browser.
Utilizes PPO training implemented via tinygrad, leveraging TinyJit and WebGPU kernels.
Highlights the trend of bringing complex ML training capabilities to the web via WebGPU.

stories.title: Mullvad exit IPs as a fingerprinting vector
stories.author: tmctmt.com via bedrovelsen
bullet_points: Mullvad is actively removing its exit IP addresses from public visibility.
The action is taken because these exit IPs can be exploited as a vector for user fingerprinting.
This addresses privacy concerns related to how VPN exit nodes can be tracked by third parties.

stories.title: Building "Sweets Vault" - a multimodal Gemini Agent with physical hardware integration
stories.author: rsamborski
bullet_points: The project involves building a multimodal Gemini Agent named 'Sweets Vault'.
The agent is designed with physical hardware integration, suggesting a tangible application.
The stated goal is to motivate young children (seven-year-olds) to complete educational tasks like reading and handwriting.

stories.title: Bigger AI models aren't always better. Here's how to actually choose.
stories.author: rohini_gaonkar
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that larger AI models inherently perform better.
Focuses on providing practical guidance for selecting appropriate AI models.
Highlights the issue of model reliability, referencing hallucination as a key differentiator.
